new_classes.definition_en: Vinegar obtained exclusively from wine (acetic fermentation of wine), classified under CN heading 2209 (Chapter 22). Includes both wine vinegar in containers of 2 litres or less (CN 22090011) and wine vinegar in containers of more than 2 litres (CN 22090019).

new_classes.label_en: Wine Vinegar in Containers of 2 Litres or Less (CN 22090011)
new_classes.label_de: Weinessig in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 Litern oder weniger (KN 22090011)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine vinegar presented in containers holding 2 litres or less, classified under CN subheading 22090011 (Chapter 22). Distinguished from CN 22090019 by the container volume criterion (≤ 2 L).

coverage_explanation: The principal distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) the product type — wine vinegar (sourced from wine via acetic fermentation, as referenced by the HS Explanatory Note to heading 2209 part (I) second paragraph (1)); and (2) the container-volume threshold (≤ 2 L) that separates CN 22090011 from its sibling CN 22090019. The legal note also references "additional note 11 to this chapter", which likely specifies further technical details (e.g. minimum acidity, fermentation substrate definition) that are not reproduced in the available text and therefore cannot be encoded. This reduces coverage from 1.0. The sibling code 22090019 (> 2 L containers) is not modelled here but would be a straightforward counterpart class.
